_meta.title: Partner Integration Lifecycle Workflow — Structured Specification
_meta.description: The assembled structured specification for the Partner Integration Lifecycle Workflow. Combines object definitions (fields and types), state definitions (valid enumerated states per field), conditional logic (if/then rules by decision point), and completion criteria (verifiable field-level done checks per step) into a single self-contained document. All cross-references between source documents have been resolved. This specification is the authoritative blueprint for building the Partner Program Configurator.
source_documents: conditional_logic.json — if/then rules by decision point (Step 4)
completion_criteria.json — verifiable done checks per step (Step 5)
assembly_notes: Field name resolutions applied: partner_record.status renamed to partner_record.intake_decision to match state_definitions canonical naming. Original name preserved in original_name_in_object_definitions field.

Missing status fields added to objects where state_definitions defined states for fields absent from object_definitions tables: lifecycle_status (partner_record), profile_status (partner_profile), spec_status (integration_plan_spec), package_status (launch_readiness_package), handoff_status (operational_handoff_package), step9_readiness (operations_record), plan_status and play_status (growth_plan), review_status (lifecycle_decision_record). All additions marked with resolution_note.
Valid states are embedded directly into field definitions where the field is an enum type with states defined in state_definitions.
Conditional logic, completion criteria, and workflow step structure are embedded in full from their respective source documents.
_meta.how_to_use: A configurator built from this specification should: (1) Present the four decision points from the decision_points section to the user. (2) Use conditional_logic.object_activation_rules to determine which objects exist. (3) Use conditional_logic.field_activation_rules to determine which fields within objects are active. (4) Use conditional_logic.progression_gate_rules (also embedded in each workflow_step.progression_gate) to enforce step sequencing. (5) Use conditional_logic.state_transition_rules (also embedded in each object.state_transitions) to track object states. (6) Use workflow_steps[step].completion_criteria to evaluate whether each step is done. (7) Use workflow_steps[step].workflow_modifications to render step-level instructions appropriate to the configuration.

DP1.question: Is there a technical integration, and if so, in which direction?

options.label: No technical integration — purely commercial relationship

DP1.structural_impact: Highest. no_integration removes Steps 3 and 5 entirely and modifies Steps 4, 6, 7, 8, and 9. Direction (entity_to_partner vs partner_to_entity vs bidirectional) changes ownership and evidence obligations within Steps 3, 4, and 5.

DP2.question: Which commercial motions does your program support?

options.label: Referral — partner refers customers to entity
Referral — entity refers customers to partner
Marketplace — listed on entity's own marketplace
Marketplace — listed on a third-party marketplace (e.g. AWS, Microsoft, Google Cloud)

DP2.note: co_marketing requires budget allocation regardless of funding source and also activates the commercial finance track in Step 4.
DP2.structural_impact: Content-rich. Each selected motion activates specific entitlements in Step 2, commercial track content in Step 4, plays in Step 9, and attribution/economics mechanics. Non-selected motions are entirely absent from the workflow.

DP3.question: Is certification required, and of what type?

options.label: Integration/technical certification only (e.g. security review, SOC 2, platform technical certification)
Partner competency certification only (partner must pass training/assessment to sell or implement)

DP3.structural_impact: Targeted. Activates/removes the Certification Record object and modifies specific elements in Steps 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, and 9.

DP4.question: Does your program operate in or serve regulated industries?

options.label: No — Compliance/Risk review track not required

DP4.structural_impact: Binary and clean. yes activates the entire Compliance/Risk track in Steps 3 and 4 and modifies go-live prerequisites. no removes it entirely.

partner_record.presence: Universal — exists for every partner regardless of decision point answers
partner_record.activation_note: Universal. Created at Step 1 for every inbound request.

fields.name: partner_type_hypothesis

fields.name: compliance_flags

fields.name: intake_decision

fields.notes: Required if status = decline. Free text.

fields.name: next_step_assignment

fields.name: os_version_at_intake

fields.name: lifecycle_status
fields.notes: Tracks partner position across the full workflow. Updated at each step transition.
fields.resolution_note: Field defined in state_definitions but absent from object_definitions table. Added during Step 6 assembly as a cross-reference resolution.

partner_profile.presence: Universal — extends Partner Record for all partners that reach Step 2 (status = engage)
partner_profile.activation_note: Created at Step 2 only for partners where intake_decision = engage.

fields.name: track
fields.notes: Partner track assigned per Step 0 criteria

fields.name: entitlements.co_marketing_eligible
fields.notes: [DP2 — co-marketing] True if co-marketing motion is selected and partner is eligible

activation_rule.when_active: Field exists and must be set to true or false per tier criteria
activation_rule.when_inactive: Field absent — co-marketing not a supported motion

fields.name: required_gates.compliance_review_required
fields.notes: [DP4 — regulated] True if program operates in regulated industries

activation_rule.when_active: Field exists and is flagged based on partner use case
activation_rule.when_inactive: Field absent — program does not operate in regulated industries

fields.name: required_gates.certification_required
fields.notes: [DP3] True if certification (either type) is required

activation_rule.when_active: Field exists — certification type determined by DP3 value

fields.name: engagement_model
fields.notes: Description of how entity and partner will work together

fields.name: motion_classification
fields.notes: Confirmed motions: referral_inbound / referral_outbound / reseller_partner / reseller_entity / marketplace_entity / marketplace_partner / marketplace_third_party / co_sell / co_marketing

fields.name: profile_status
fields.notes: Tracks completeness and validity of the partner profile.
fields.resolution_note: Field defined in state_definitions but absent from object_definitions table. Added during Step 6 assembly as a cross-reference resolution.

integration_plan_spec.presence: Conditional — **ABSENT if Decision Point 1 = no technical integration**. Present for all other DP1 answers.
integration_plan_spec.activation_note: Absent entirely if DP1 = no_integration. Created at Step 3 for all other DP1 answers.

activation_rule.note: Entire object absent if DP1 = no_integration, so this field is always present when object exists

fields.name: integration_path
fields.notes: certified / supported / partner_built. "certified" only available if DP3 ≠ neither

activation_rule.note: 'certified' option only available when DP3 = integration_cert_only or both

fields.name: scope.data_flow_description
fields.notes: What data moves, in which direction, retention

fields.name: dependencies.legal_prerequisites
fields.notes: Legal requirements that must be met before build

fields.name: owners
fields.notes: Named owners for the integration work

fields.name: partner_confirmed_intent
fields.notes: Partner has confirmed intent to proceed (required for handoff to Step 4)

fields.name: spec_status
fields.notes: Tracks the lifecycle of the integration plan/spec through scoping, approval, and rescoping.
fields.resolution_note: Field defined in state_definitions but absent from object_definitions table. Added during Step 6 assembly as a cross-reference resolution.

approval_record.presence: Universal — all four tracks are defined here, but the Compliance/Risk track sub-fields are conditional on DP4, and the Commercial/Finance track sub-fields are conditional on DP2
approval_record.activation_note: Universal. Created at Step 4. Internal tracks are conditional — see field_activation_rules.

fields.name: security_privacy.conditions
fields.notes: Required if status = conditional_approval. Each condition must have an owner and deadline.

fields.name: security_privacy.approved_version
fields.notes: Version of scope/spec that was approved

fields.name: security_privacy.preliminary_approval_granted
fields.notes: Approval to begin non-production work (minimum-to-unblock gate)

fields.name: security_privacy.no_go_risks_resolved
fields.notes: All hard no-go risks resolved (minimum-to-unblock gate)

fields.name: compliance_risk.conditions
fields.notes: [DP4] Required if status = conditional_approval. Each condition must have an owner and deadline.

fields.name: compliance_risk.preliminary_approval_granted
fields.notes: [DP4] Preliminary approval or defined constraints for implementation to proceed

fields.name: compliance_risk.final_approval_granted
fields.notes: [DP4] Final sign-off required for production go-live

fields.name: legal.agreement_type
fields.notes: standard_partnership / integration_agreement / reseller_agreement / marketplace_agreement / combined — value determined by DP2 motion selections

fields.name: legal.agreement_path_approved
fields.notes: Approved agreement path (template selected + redline boundaries agreed) — minimum-to-unblock gate

fields.name: legal.conditions
fields.notes: Required if status = conditional_approval. Each condition must have an owner and deadline.

fields.name: commercial_finance.economics_confirmed
fields.notes: [DP2] Deal economics (pricing, fee definitions, payout triggers) confirmed

fields.name: commercial_finance.payout_mechanics_confirmed
fields.notes: [DP2] Billing/invoicing/payout mechanics confirmed — required for Step 8→9 handoff gate

fields.name: commercial_finance.exceptions_approved
fields.notes: [DP2] Any commercial exceptions approved per Step 0 policy

fields.name: minimum_to_unblock_met
fields.notes: Derived: true when security_privacy.preliminary_approval_granted = true AND legal.agreement_path_approved = true AND security_privacy.no_go_risks_resolved = true AND (if DP4: compliance_risk.preliminary_approval_granted = true)

fields.name: go_live_criteria_met
fields.notes: Derived: true when security_privacy.final_approval_granted = true AND legal.executed_agreement = true AND (if DP4: compliance_risk.final_approval_granted = true) AND (if DP2 financial motion: commercial_finance.economics_confirmed = true)

launch_readiness_package.created_at_step: Steps 5 and 6 — Step 5 produces the implementation inputs; Step 6 assembles and completes the full package
launch_readiness_package.presence: Universal — always produced, but contents vary by DP1, DP2, and DP3
launch_readiness_package.activation_note: Universal. Contents vary by DP1, DP2, DP3 — see field_activation_rules.

fields.name: test_results_provided
fields.notes: Test results and evidence from implementation

fields.name: integration_meets_spec
fields.notes: Solutions Engineering confirmation that integration meets approved spec

fields.name: certification_evidence_confirmed
fields.notes: [DP3 — integration certification] Certification artifacts complete and accepted. Absent if DP3 = neither or partner_competency_only

activation_rule.when_active: Field required — certification evidence must be confirmed before Step 6 completes
activation_rule.when_inactive: Field absent — no integration certification required

fields.name: integration_guide_complete
fields.notes: [DP1 — technical integration present] Integration guide complete. Absent if DP1 = no technical integration

activation_rule.when_active: Field required — integration guide is a mandatory launch kit deliverable
activation_rule.when_inactive: Field absent — no technical integration to document

fields.name: marketplace_listing_ready

fields.name: brand_legal_compliance_approved

activation_rule.when_active: Field required (not optional) — public-facing joint assets require brand/legal/compliance approval
activation_rule.when_inactive: Field present but optional — applies only if launch comms involve public-facing claims

fields.name: contacts_confirmed

fields.name: handoff_status

fields.resolution_note: Field defined in state_definitions but absent from object_definitions table. Added during Step 6 assembly as a cross-reference resolution.

operations_record.presence: Universal — always produced, but health metric sub-fields vary by DP1

fields.resolution_note: Field defined in state_definitions but absent from object_definitions table. Added during Step 6 assembly as a cross-reference resolution.

certification_record.created_at_step: Step 2 (gating condition established) and Step 5 (for integration cert) or Step 2 ongoing (for partner competency cert)

progression_gate.description: Partner Operating System must be current before Step 1 can proceed

completion_criteria.accepting_party: Partnerships (Partner Development / Partner Ops) — accepts the current OS as the basis for Step 1 intake
outputs_produced: partner_operating_system record with status = current
Published rules, templates, and configured systems
Versioned change log entry
Governance calendar and role/accountability map

blocking_conditions.effect: Step 1 progression paused until hotfix is folded into next versioned release
Step 1 progression paused until new version is published and communicated

completion_criteria.conditional_variations: None — Step 0 is universal and not affected by any decision point answer

progression_gate.description: Step 1 complete — partner record has intake decision and owner assigned
progression_gate.condition: partner_record.intake_decision IN ['engage', 'decline', 'park'] AND partner_record.owner != null AND partner_record.lifecycle_status != 'pending_os'

completion_criteria.done_label: Partner record created with intake decision and owner assigned

terminal_outcomes.effect: Record closed. partner_record.decline_reason must be populated. No further progression.
Record open and held. No progression until re-engagement decision.

completion_criteria.accepting_party: Partner Programs / Partnerships Ops — accepts the engaged partner record for Step 2 placement
outputs_produced: partner_record with intake_decision, status, owner, and next_step_assignment populated

blocking_conditions.effect: Blocks progression to Step 2 until resolved
Blocks progression to Step 2 until resolved
Halts progression — record remains open
Blocks intake entirely — partner_record.lifecycle_status = pending_os

completion_criteria.conditional_variations: None — Step 1 is universal and not affected by any decision point answer

step10_finance_revops_authority.when_financial_motion_selected: Finance/RevOps has final decision authority on economics viability at renewal/re-tier/exit review.
step10_finance_revops_authority.when_no_financial_motion: Finance/RevOps authority at Step 10 is limited to general commercial constraints — no payout economics to adjudicate.

progression_gate.description: Step 2 complete — partner profile has track, tier, entitlements, gates, and engagement model
progression_gate.condition: partner_profile.profile_status == 'current' AND partner_profile.track != null AND partner_profile.tier != null AND partner_profile.engagement_model != null
progression_gate.when_DP1_is_no_integration: Handoff goes directly to Step 4 (Step 3 skipped)
blocked_by: unmet_access_prerequisites == true (security/compliance gate or contracting prerequisite not satisfied)

DP3 IN ['partner_cert_only', 'both'] AND certification_as_prerequisite_to_step3 == true AND certification_record.partner_cert.status != 'certified' (partner must achieve certification before proceeding, or certification may be set as concurrent — determined by program configuration)

completion_criteria.done_label: Partner profile complete with track, tier, entitlements, required gates, and engagement model
completion_criteria.accepting_party: Solutions Engineering / Partner Engineering (when DP1 has integration) or Partnerships Deal Desk / Program Manager (when DP1 = no_integration) — accepts the partner profile for the next step
outputs_produced: partner_profile with track, tier, entitlements, required_gates, engagement_model, and motion_classification populated
certification_record created (if DP3 != neither)
Entitlement assignments per selected commercial motions (see conditional_variations)

blocking_conditions.effect: Blocks progression until security review is completed
Blocks sandbox access and may block Step 3 progression

blocking_conditions.condition: DP4 == 'yes' AND partner_profile.required_gates.compliance_review_required == true AND compliance_condition_not_met == true
blocking_conditions.effect: Blocks progression until compliance condition is satisfied

blocking_conditions.condition: DP3 IN ['partner_cert_only', 'both'] AND certification_as_prerequisite_to_step3 == true AND certification_record.partner_cert.status != 'certified'
blocking_conditions.effect: Blocks progression to Step 3 until partner achieves certification (if configured as gate) — may be concurrent instead depending on program configuration
Blocks progression until revision is complete

conditional_variations.when_DP1_is_no_integration: Handoff routes to Step 4 directly. Step 3 does not activate.
conditional_variations.when_DP2_includes_referral_inbound: partner_profile.entitlements must include referral tracking access and lead submission mechanism
conditional_variations.when_DP2_includes_referral_outbound: partner_profile.entitlements must include entity referral process definition
conditional_variations.when_DP2_includes_reseller_partner: partner_profile.entitlements must include margin/discount structure, deal registration access, and reseller-specific SLAs
conditional_variations.when_DP2_includes_reseller_entity: partner_profile.entitlements must include entity access to partner product for resale and reverse margin/commission structure
conditional_variations.when_DP2_includes_any_marketplace: partner_profile.entitlements must include marketplace listing access and platform-specific prerequisites
conditional_variations.when_DP2_includes_co_sell: partner_profile.entitlements must include account mapping access, joint pipeline tooling access, and lead-sharing rights
conditional_variations.when_DP2_includes_co_marketing: partner_profile.entitlements.co_marketing_eligible must be set to true or false

step2_entitlements_by_motion._description: Step 2 entitlement assignments vary by selected commercial motion. Each motion adds specific entitlements to the partner profile.
step2_entitlements_by_motion.when_referral_inbound_selected: Partner receives referral tracking access and lead submission mechanism. Referral entitlements recorded in partner_profile.entitlements.
step2_entitlements_by_motion.when_referral_outbound_selected: Entity referral process defined — how entity identifies and passes leads to partner. Reverse referral terms recorded in partner_profile.entitlements.
step2_entitlements_by_motion.when_reseller_partner_selected: Partner receives margin/discount structure, deal registration access, and reseller-specific SLAs. Reseller entitlements recorded in partner_profile.entitlements.
step2_entitlements_by_motion.when_reseller_entity_selected: Entity receives access to partner product for resale. Margin/commission structure from partner to entity recorded in partner_profile.entitlements.
step2_entitlements_by_motion.when_marketplace_any_selected: Listing entitlements assigned — marketplace access, listing slot, and any platform-specific prerequisites recorded in partner_profile.entitlements.
step2_entitlements_by_motion.when_co_sell_selected: Co-sell entitlements assigned — account mapping access, joint pipeline tooling access, and lead-sharing rights recorded in partner_profile.entitlements.
step2_entitlements_by_motion.when_co_marketing_selected: Co-marketing eligibility flag set (partner_profile.entitlements.co_marketing_eligible = true). Budget access and campaign participation rights recorded.

step_3.step_name: Integration Request Triage, Deep Scoping, Commitment

progression_gate.description: Step 3 complete — integration plan/spec is approved and partner has confirmed intent
blocked_by: integration_plan_spec.spec_status == 'rejected' (no path forward — partner exits or parks)
DP4 == 'yes' AND compliance_risk_prohibited_use_identified == true (blocked until rescoped)

completion_criteria.step_name: Integration Request Triage, Deep Scoping, Commitment
completion_criteria.applicability: Conditional — this step is ABSENT when DP1 = no_integration. When absent, Step 2 handoff routes directly to Step 4.
completion_criteria.done_label: Integration plan/spec approved and partner has confirmed intent to proceed
completion_criteria.accepting_party: Partnerships Deal Desk / Program Manager — accepts the approved spec to initiate parallel approval tracks in Step 4
outputs_produced: integration_plan_spec with spec_status = approved and all scope fields populated

blocking_conditions.effect: Scoping clock paused — partner must re-confirm intent within defined timebox. If timebox expires: spec_status → rejected, partner_record.lifecycle_status → dormant.
Progression halted. deferral_revisit_date must be set. Partner parks until revisit.
No viable integration path. Partner exits or parks.
Blocks handoff to Step 4 until Product approves scope and roadmap commitment
Blocks handoff to Step 4. Must return to Step 3 for rescope.
Blocks handoff to Step 4. Must return to Step 3 for rescope.

terminal_outcomes.effect: No viable integration path identified. Document reason. Partner exits or parks. Missing standards/templates routed to Step 0.

conditional_variations.when_DP1_is_entity_to_partner: integration_plan_spec.integration_direction = entity_to_partner. Scoping centers on partner APIs and endpoints. Entity engineering owns build commitment.
conditional_variations.when_DP1_is_partner_to_entity: integration_plan_spec.integration_direction = partner_to_entity. Scoping centers on entity APIs and endpoints. Partner engineering owns build. Entity scopes and commits only.
conditional_variations.when_DP1_is_bidirectional: integration_plan_spec.integration_direction = bidirectional. Scope must cover both directions. Two build streams defined.
conditional_variations.when_DP3_is_neither: integration_plan_spec.integration_path valid values: ['supported', 'partner_built'] only. 'certified' is not available.
conditional_variations.when_DP3_includes_integration_cert: integration_plan_spec.integration_path valid values: ['certified', 'supported', 'partner_built']. If certified is selected, certification is a required deliverable — not optional.
conditional_variations.when_DP4_is_yes: Compliance/Risk tie-breaker authority is active. Compliance/Risk can block handoff to Step 4 if prohibited use or policy constraint is identified.

step3_scoping_ownership.when_DP1_is_entity_to_partner: Scoping centers on partner APIs and endpoints. Entity engineering team owns build commitment.
step3_scoping_ownership.when_DP1_is_partner_to_entity: Scoping centers on entity APIs and endpoints. Partner engineering team owns build. Entity Solutions Engineering scopes and commits only.
step3_scoping_ownership.when_DP1_is_bidirectional: Scoping covers both directions simultaneously. Both build streams must be defined.

progression_gate.description: Step 4 minimum-to-unblock criteria met — implementation may begin
progression_gate.minimum_to_unblock_formula_when_DP4_yes: security_privacy.preliminary_approval_granted == true AND security_privacy.no_go_risks_resolved == true AND legal.status IN ['agreement_path_approved', 'executed'] AND compliance_risk.preliminary_approval_granted == true
progression_gate.minimum_to_unblock_formula_when_DP4_no: security_privacy.preliminary_approval_granted == true AND security_privacy.no_go_risks_resolved == true AND legal.status IN ['agreement_path_approved', 'executed']
progression_gate.when_DP1_is_no_integration: Step 5 is skipped — gate_step4_to_step6_no_implementation applies instead
blocked_by: any_track.status == 'escalated' (Step 5 work paused until escalation resolves)

completion_criteria.done_label_for_step5_start: Minimum-to-unblock criteria met — implementation may begin
completion_criteria.done_label_for_step6_start: Go-live criteria met — production launch may proceed

done_condition_minimum_to_unblock.description: Conditions required before Step 5 (implementation) may begin

checks.description: All conditional_approval conditions must have named owners and deadlines documented
All conditional_approval conditions must have named owners and deadlines documented

done_condition_go_live.description: Conditions required before production go-live (Step 6/7) may proceed

completion_criteria.accepting_party_for_step5: Solutions Engineering / Partner Engineering — accepts minimum-to-unblock confirmation to begin implementation
completion_criteria.accepting_party_for_step6_when_no_integr: Partnerships (Partner Enablement / Partner Ops) — accepts go-live criteria confirmation directly (Step 5 skipped when DP1 = no_integration)
outputs_produced: approval_record with all required track statuses and derived gate fields populated
Executed agreement(s) or approved near-final form
Documented approval record: approvers, versions, conditions with owners and deadlines

blocking_conditions.effect: Return to Step 3 for rescope or downgrade integration path. If not feasible: park/exit.
Step 5 work paused. Escalation owner must resolve or close effort.
Return to Step 3 for rescope. If not feasible: park/exit.
Step 5 work paused. Escalation owner must resolve or close effort.
Blocks progression. Escalation required per Step 0 governance. If unresolved: park/exit.
Partner exits or parks.
Step 5 work paused. Escalation owner must resolve or close effort.
Indefinite conditional approvals are prohibited. Conditions must have owners and deadlines before Step 5 begins.

conditional_variations.when_DP1_is_no_integration: Security & Privacy track scope is significantly reduced — limited to data handling obligations in legal agreement only. No data-flow review, auth/encryption controls, or security testing. Step 5 is skipped — go-live criteria must be fully met before Step 6 begins.
conditional_variations.when_DP3_includes_integration_cert: security_privacy track: security testing requirements are REQUIRED (not conditional). Specific certification standard and evidence requirements must be defined as explicit gate conditions.
conditional_variations.when_DP4_is_yes: compliance_risk track is fully active and required. preliminary_approval_granted must be true before Step 5 begins. final_approval_granted must be true before go-live.
conditional_variations.when_DP4_is_no: compliance_risk track is entirely absent from approval_record.
conditional_variations.when_DP2_has_financial_motion: commercial_finance track is fully active. All sub-items required. commercial_finance.payout_mechanics_confirmed is a hard gate for Step 8→9 handoff.
conditional_variations.when_DP2_has_no_financial_motion: commercial_finance track is absent from approval_record.
conditional_variations.when_DP2_includes_marketplace_third_part: Additional marketplace platform agreement required in addition to partner agreement. legal track must confirm both are executed.

step4_legal_agreement_type.when_reseller_partner_selected: legal.agreement_type = 'reseller_agreement' — covers resale rights, territory, pricing floors
step4_legal_agreement_type.when_marketplace_third_party_selected: Additional marketplace platform agreement required (e.g. AWS Marketplace Seller Agreement, Microsoft Publisher Agreement) in addition to partner agreement

progression_gate.description: Step 5 complete — integration meets spec and launch-readiness inputs are provided
progression_gate.condition: DP1 != 'no_integration' AND launch_readiness_package.integration_meets_spec == true AND launch_readiness_package.test_results_provided == true AND launch_readiness_package.known_limitations_documented == true AND launch_readiness_package.support_contacts_confirmed == true

completion_criteria.applicability: Conditional — this step is ABSENT when DP1 = no_integration. When absent, Step 4 handoff routes directly to Step 6.
completion_criteria.done_label: Working integration confirmed against approved spec with launch-readiness inputs provided
completion_criteria.accepting_party: Partnerships (Partner Enablement / Partner Ops) — accepts the launch-readiness inputs to begin Step 6 packaging
outputs_produced: launch_readiness_package with Step 5 input fields populated (integration_meets_spec, test_results_provided, known_limitations_documented, support_contacts_confirmed)
certification_record.integration_cert.status = achieved (if DP3 includes integration cert)

blocking_conditions.effect: Blocks handoff to Step 6 until Product grants functional acceptance
Blocks handoff to Step 6 until evidence/controls requirements are met
Blocks handoff to Step 6 until certification is achieved
Work paused. Close as Dormant. partner_record.lifecycle_status → dormant. Re-entry requires Step 3 re-scope.
Do not proceed to launch. Remain in Step 5 until conditions are met or formally de-scope/exit.
Return to Step 3 for rescope and/or Step 4 for revised conditions as needed.

conditional_variations.when_DP1_is_entity_to_partner: Entity leads implementation. Partner provides access credentials, sandbox, and API documentation.
conditional_variations.when_DP1_is_partner_to_entity: Partner leads implementation. Entity provides access credentials, sandbox, and API documentation. Evidence collection is partner's responsibility, validated by entity.
conditional_variations.when_DP1_is_bidirectional: Two parallel build streams coordinated simultaneously. Both parties lead their respective implementations.
conditional_variations.when_DP3_is_neither: certification deliverable is absent. Step 5 completes with test results and evidence only.
conditional_variations.when_DP3_includes_integration_cert: certification_record.integration_cert.status must reach 'achieved' before handoff to Step 6. launch_readiness_package.certification_evidence_confirmed must be true.

step5_implementation_ownership.when_DP1_is_entity_to_partner: Entity leads implementation. Partner provides access credentials, sandbox, and API documentation.
step5_implementation_ownership.when_DP1_is_partner_to_entity: Partner leads implementation. Entity provides access credentials, sandbox, and API documentation. Evidence collection is partner's responsibility, validated by entity.
step5_implementation_ownership.when_DP1_is_bidirectional: Two parallel build streams coordinated. Both parties lead their respective implementations.

progression_gate.description: Step 6 complete — launch artifacts are complete and go-live prerequisites are satisfied
progression_gate.go_live_criteria_formula_when_DP4_yes_an: security_privacy.final_approval_granted == true AND legal.status == 'executed' AND compliance_risk.final_approval_granted == true AND commercial_finance.economics_confirmed == true
progression_gate.go_live_criteria_formula_when_DP4_yes_no: security_privacy.final_approval_granted == true AND legal.status == 'executed' AND compliance_risk.final_approval_granted == true
progression_gate.go_live_criteria_formula_when_DP4_no_and: security_privacy.final_approval_granted == true AND legal.status == 'executed' AND commercial_finance.economics_confirmed == true

completion_criteria.done_label: Launch package complete and required approvals for partner-facing/public assets obtained

when_DP3_includes_partner_cert.description: Partner-facing enablement must include certification program materials: training content, assessment details, certification pathway, and badge information

completion_criteria.accepting_party: Engineering (integration owner / SRE-on-point) — accepts the complete launch package to schedule cutover in Step 7
outputs_produced: launch_readiness_package with package_status = complete and all required fields confirmed
Partner-facing launch kit
Internal support playbook and escalation procedures
Sales/CS notes
Launch comms plan with approvals
Marketplace listing (if applicable)
Certification materials in enablement package (if DP3 includes partner cert)

blocking_conditions.effect: Do not proceed to Step 7. Complete missing deliverables or route fixes to Step 5.
Blocks handoff to Step 7. Return to Step 4 if approvals/evidence are missing.
Blocks cutover scheduling until Engineering confirms readiness.
Blocks go-live if listing is required. May proceed without listing only if explicitly acceptable per governance.
Blocks public-facing assets and announcements. Timebox revisions or postpone launch comms.
Do not proceed to Step 7.

conditional_variations.when_DP1_is_no_integration: Launch kit covers commercial motion content only — referral submission process, reseller ordering, marketplace listing usage. integration_guide_complete field is absent.
conditional_variations.when_DP1_has_integration: Launch kit must include integration_guide_complete = true in addition to all universal deliverables.
conditional_variations.when_DP2_includes_co_marketing_or_market: brand_legal_compliance_approved is required (not optional) — public-facing joint assets mandate formal approval.
conditional_variations.when_DP3_includes_partner_cert: Enablement packaging must include certification program materials.
conditional_variations.when_DP3_includes_integration_cert: Launch readiness checklist must confirm certification_evidence_confirmed = true.

step6_launch_kit_content.when_DP1_is_no_integration: Launch kit covers commercial motion only — referral submission process, reseller ordering, marketplace listing usage. No integration guide.
step6_launch_kit_content.when_DP1_has_integration: Launch kit includes integration guide, technical documentation, and known technical limitations in addition to commercial content.
step6_launch_kit_content.when_DP3_includes_partner_cert: Launch kit includes certification program materials — training content, assessment details, certification pathway, badge information.

progression_gate.description: Step 7 complete — hypercare exit criteria met and formal handoff confirmed
progression_gate.prerequisites: operational_handoff_package.hypercare_exit_criteria_met == true AND operational_handoff_package.runbooks_confirmed == true AND operational_handoff_package.contacts_confirmed == true AND no_unresolved_critical_issues == true
blocked_by: operational_handoff_package.handoff_status == 'rolled_back' (must return to Step 5 and Step 6 first)

completion_criteria.done_label: Hypercare exit criteria met and formal handoff to Support/Operations confirmed

checks.description: No unresolved critical issues at time of handoff acceptance

completion_criteria.accepting_party: Support / Operations — accepts the operational handoff package as the new owner of steady-state SLAs and incident response
outputs_produced: operational_handoff_package with handoff_status = handed_off
Live integration with stabilized baseline (if DP1 has integration)
Confirmed operational handoff

blocking_conditions.effect: Support/Operations refuses handoff. Missing runbooks, contacts, or unresolved critical issues must be resolved.
Go-live rolled back. Open incident. Extend hypercare. Return to Step 5 for fixes. Refresh Step 6 readiness artifacts before re-attempting Step 7.
Sev1/critical incident active. Freeze further rollout. Hypercare exit blocked until stability criteria are met after incident resolution.
Postpone launch. Return to Step 6 (and Step 5 as needed) before rescheduling Step 7.
Return to Step 6 and/or Step 5.

conditional_variations.when_DP1_is_no_integration: No technical cutover. rollback_plan_ready and rollback_executed fields are absent. Hypercare monitors commercial motion activation only (first referral submitted, first listing live, first reseller order).
conditional_variations.when_DP1_has_integration: Full technical hypercare — monitoring, rapid triage, rollback plan readiness. Engineering leads cutover decision and rollback authority.

step7_hypercare_scope.when_DP1_is_no_integration: Hypercare monitors commercial motion activation only — first referral submitted, first listing live, first reseller order. No technical rollback plan.
step7_hypercare_scope.when_DP1_has_integration: Full technical hypercare — monitoring, rapid triage, rollback plan readiness. Engineering leads.

progression_gate.description: Step 8 activation signals met and Step 9 readiness confirmed
progression_gate.step9_readiness_formula_with_financial_m: operations_record.stability_status == 'stable' AND operations_record.support_burden_level == 'acceptable' AND operations_record.activation_signals_met == true AND operations_record.payout_mechanics_ready == true
progression_gate.step9_readiness_formula_no_financial_mot: operations_record.stability_status == 'stable' AND operations_record.support_burden_level == 'acceptable' AND operations_record.activation_signals_met == true

completion_criteria.done_label: Integration is stable, activation signals are met, and Step 9 readiness is confirmed
completion_criteria.note: Step 8 does not have a single terminal done condition — it is an ongoing operational state. The done condition below applies specifically to the handoff gate that activates Step 9.

activation_signals.description: At least one of the following entry triggers must be satisfied (operations_record.activation_signals_met = true)

gates.condition: usage crosses defined threshold or shows sustained growth
partner-sourced or partner-influenced pipeline exceeds defined threshold
partner unlocks a priority segment, geo, vertical, or platform distribution channel

activation_signals.note: Threshold values for all gates are defined in the Partner Operating System (Step 0) and are not set here. This step evaluates against those thresholds.

completion_criteria.accepting_party: Partnerships (Partner GTM / Strategic Partnerships) — accepts the step9_readiness confirmation to initiate growth plays
outputs_produced: operations_record with stability_status, support_burden_level, and step9_readiness populated
Stable operations
Improved docs and tooling
Prioritized improvement backlog

blocking_conditions.effect: Step 9 cannot activate. Growth motions blocked until stability is restored.
Step 9 growth motions paused. Remediation plan required. May trigger re-tier recommendation for Step 10.
Step 9 blocked until stability is restored.
Hard gate. Step 9 cannot activate until RevOps/Finance confirms attribution and payout mechanics are ready.

conditional_variations.when_DP1_is_no_integration: Health metrics limited to commercial indicators — volume, referral quality, pipeline accuracy, listing performance. operations_record.health_metrics.uptime, error_rate, and latency fields are absent.
conditional_variations.when_DP1_has_integration: Full health metrics active — uptime, error rates, volume, latency.
conditional_variations.when_DP2_has_financial_motion: operations_record.payout_mechanics_ready field exists and is a hard gate for Step 9 activation.
conditional_variations.when_no_financial_motion: operations_record.payout_mechanics_ready field is absent. Step 9 activates without RevOps gate.

step8_health_metrics_scope.when_DP1_is_no_integration: Health metrics focus on commercial indicators — referral quality, pipeline accuracy, listing performance, support burden.
step8_health_metrics_scope.when_DP1_has_integration: Health metrics include uptime, error rates, volume, and latency in addition to commercial indicators.

progression_gate.description: Step 9 complete — plays executed, outcomes recorded, post-play review done

completion_criteria.applicability: Conditional — this step activates only when operations_record.step9_readiness == 'ready'
completion_criteria.done_label: All selected plays executed, outcomes recorded, and post-play review complete

checks.description: All play_status fields for selected plays must be == 'complete'
Executed plays, outcomes, and any re-tier/remediation recommendations recorded by Partnerships GTM

expansion.condition: Expansion play is always present when Step 9 activates. Fields vary by DP1.

checks.description: growth_plan.expansion.new_use_case_activations must be non-empty if new use case activation was a selected play objective
growth_plan.expansion.new_segment_rollout must be populated if new segment rollout was a selected play objective

when_DP1_has_integration.note: Must be explicitly set to true (expansion included deeper integration) or false (it did not) — null is not acceptable as a done state

completion_criteria.accepting_party: Partnerships (Partner Management) — accepts recorded outcomes and recommendations for Step 10 QBR/annual review
outputs_produced: growth_plan with plan_status = complete and post_play_review_complete = true
KPI tracking: pipeline created/influenced, conversion, adoption/activation, attach rate, support burden impact
Post-play review with recommendations routed to Step 0

blocking_conditions.effect: Specific play paused. Other plays may continue if unaffected. Overall completion blocked until paused play resolves or is formally closed.
Pause co-sell play. Resolve ownership per sales governance before restarting.
Return to Step 8 to stabilize. Route systemic fixes to Step 0.
Follow defined dispute process. Freeze credit/payouts for contested items. Resolve via RevOps/Finance escalation.
Delays handoff to Step 10 — performance, economics, or risk inputs from RevOps/Finance, Support/Operations, or Security/Compliance must be available.

conditional_variations.when_DP1_is_no_integration: growth_plan.expansion.deeper_integration_depth field is absent. Expansion plays limited to new use case activation, new segment rollout, and enablement refresh.
conditional_variations.when_DP1_has_integration: All expansion plays available including deeper integration depth.
conditional_variations.when_DP2_includes_co_sell: co_sell sub-object is present. Direction determines account ownership, close-plan ownership, and credit attribution configuration.
conditional_variations.when_DP2_includes_co_marketing: co_marketing sub-object is present. budget_source and budget_approved must be confirmed.
conditional_variations.when_DP2_includes_marketplace: marketplace sub-object is present. listing_quality_confirmed, release_notes_current, and promotion_schedule_defined must all be true.
conditional_variations.when_DP2_has_financial_motion: attribution sub-object is present and all attribution fields must be confirmed. RevOps/Finance has final authority on attribution, payout eligibility, and reconciliation.
conditional_variations.when_both_referral_directions_selected: Attribution records must include direction field (partner_to_entity OR entity_to_partner) on every referral credit. Reporting aggregates by direction separately.

step9_co_sell_account_ownership.when_direction_is_entity_led: Account ownership resides with entity. Partner has no independent claim. Credit: entity-sourced, partner-influenced.
step9_co_sell_account_ownership.when_direction_is_partner_led: Account ownership resides with partner for duration of deal. Rules for post-deal account access must be defined. Credit: partner-sourced, entity-influenced. Account conflict risk: highest — explicit rules required if entity direct sales encounters same account.
step9_co_sell_account_ownership.when_direction_is_jointly_led: Account ownership is shared. Rules must address renewal, upsell, and support escalation ownership post-deal. Credit: jointly sourced — split rules defined upfront in CRM/PRM. Weekly pipeline review required.

step9_expansion_play_scope.when_DP1_is_no_integration: Expansion plays: new use case activation, new segment rollout, enablement refresh. 'Deeper integration depth (additional endpoints/modules)' is absent.
step9_expansion_play_scope.when_DP1_has_integration: All expansion plays available including deeper integration depth.

step9_referral_attribution_direction._description: When both referral_inbound and referral_outbound are selected simultaneously, attribution mechanics must distinguish between the two flows to prevent double-counting and reporting confusion.
step9_referral_attribution_direction.when_only_referral_inbound_selected: Attribution tracks partner-to-entity referrals only. Credit definitions: partner-sourced. Single attribution flow — no directional disambiguation required.
step9_referral_attribution_direction.when_only_referral_outbound_selected: Attribution tracks entity-to-partner referrals only. Credit definitions: entity-sourced. Reverse referral fee or quid pro quo tracked. Single attribution flow.
step9_referral_attribution_direction.when_both_referral_directions_selected: Attribution mechanics must explicitly tag each credit record with direction: partner_to_entity OR entity_to_partner. CRM/PRM system fields must include a direction field on every referral credit record. Reporting must aggregate by direction separately before combining totals. Payout triggers and schedules may differ by direction and must be defined independently. Dispute process must specify which direction the dispute applies to.

progression_gate.description: Step 10 review complete — decision recorded with rationale and owners
progression_gate.condition: lifecycle_decision_record.review_status == 'complete' AND lifecycle_decision_record.decision != null AND lifecycle_decision_record.rationale != null

completion_criteria.step_name: Periodic Review: Renew / Re-tier / Exit (and Program Feedback)
completion_criteria.done_label: Review complete with decision recorded, rationale documented, owners assigned, and program improvement recommendations routed to Step 0

checks.description: Program improvement recommendations routed to Step 0 for versioned updates

when_decision_is_renew.post_completion: partner_record.lifecycle_status returns to operations for new term

when_decision_is_re_tier.post_completion: partner_profile.profile_status transitions to under_revision. New entitlements agreed.

when_decision_is_remediate.post_completion: Remediation plan issued. Re-evaluation date set. May trigger return to Step 3 if integration changes required.

additional_when_customer_risk_true.description: Timeboxed remediation or migration plan must be completed before termination. Customer communications coordinated.

completion_criteria.accepting_party: Step 0 (Partner Operating System) — accepts program improvement recommendations for versioned updates
outputs_produced: lifecycle_decision_record with review_status = complete, decision populated, rationale and owners documented
Renewal paperwork, amendments, or termination notices (as applicable per decision)
Exit or remediation plan with milestones and communications plan (as applicable)
Program improvement recommendations routed to Step 0

blocking_conditions.effect: Review cannot complete. Required performance, economics, or risk inputs from RevOps/Finance, Support/Operations, or Security/Compliance must be provided.
Escalate per governance. Proceed with default decision path (renew/exit) per policy. Begin contingency planning for exit.
Document rationale. Offer remediation path with measurable criteria and deadlines. Re-evaluate only upon completion.

conditional_variations.when_DP2_has_financial_motion: Finance/RevOps has final decision authority on economics viability. Required economics inputs must be available before review_status can reach complete.
conditional_variations.when_no_financial_motion: Finance/RevOps authority at Step 10 limited to general commercial constraints.
conditional_variations.when_decision_is_remediate_and_integrati: Triggers return to Step 3 for re-triage/scoping, then Steps 4–7 as needed.

step10_finance_revops_authority.when_financial_motion_selected: Finance/RevOps has final decision authority on economics viability at renewal/re-tier/exit review.
step10_finance_revops_authority.when_no_financial_motion: Finance/RevOps authority at Step 10 is limited to general commercial constraints — no payout economics to adjudicate.

_meta.title: Partner Integration Lifecycle Workflow — Conditional Logic
_meta.description: Explicit if/then rules governing which objects, fields, states, and workflow elements are active based on decision point answers. Rules are applied additively: start with the universal baseline, then layer each decision point answer on top. Rules reference objects, fields, and states defined in the object_definitions and state_definitions documents.

decision_points.DP1: Is there a technical integration, and if so, in which direction?
decision_points.DP2: Which commercial motions does your program support? (multi-select with direction sub-choices)
decision_points.DP3: Is certification required, and of what type?
decision_points.DP4: Does your program operate in or serve regulated industries?

object_activation_rules._description: Rules governing whether an object exists at all. Objects not activated by these rules are absent from the workflow entirely.

partner_operating_system.note: Universal. Gates Step 1 activation via status field.

partner_record.note: Universal. Created at Step 1 for every inbound request.

partner_profile.note: Created at Step 2 only for partners where intake_decision = engage.

integration_plan_spec.note: Absent entirely if DP1 = no_integration. Created at Step 3 for all other DP1 answers.

approval_record.note: Universal. Created at Step 4. Internal tracks are conditional — see field_activation_rules.

launch_readiness_package.note: Universal. Contents vary by DP1, DP2, DP3 — see field_activation_rules.

operational_handoff_package.note: Universal. Some fields conditional on DP1 — see field_activation_rules.

operations_record.note: Universal. Some health metric fields conditional on DP1 — see field_activation_rules.

growth_plan.note: Conditional. Created only when Step 9 activates. Contents vary by DP1 and DP2.

lifecycle_decision_record.note: Universal. Created at each Step 10 review cycle.

certification_record.note: Absent entirely if DP3 = neither. Created when DP3 = integration_cert_only, partner_cert_only, or both.

field_activation_rules._description: Rules governing whether specific fields within objects are active. Fields not activated are absent — they do not appear as empty or null, they simply do not exist in that configuration.

entitlements.co_marketing_eligible.when_active: Field exists and must be set to true or false per tier criteria
entitlements.co_marketing_eligible.when_inactive: Field absent — co-marketing not a supported motion

required_gates.compliance_review_require.when_active: Field exists and is flagged based on partner use case
required_gates.compliance_review_require.when_inactive: Field absent — program does not operate in regulated industries

required_gates.certification_required.when_active: Field exists — certification type determined by DP3 value

integration_direction.note: Entire object absent if DP1 = no_integration, so this field is always present when object exists

integration_path.note: 'certified' option only available when DP3 = integration_cert_only or both

commercial_finance_track.note: Co-marketing alone activates this track for budget mechanics even without direct financial payments

security_testing_required.when_active: security_privacy.security_testing_requirements = required (not 'as applicable'). Specific certification standard and evidence requirements must be defined.
security_testing_required.when_inactive: security_privacy.security_testing_requirements = conditional — applied only if security review flags a need

minimum_to_unblock_met_formula.when_DP4_is_yes: security_privacy.preliminary_approval_granted == true AND security_privacy.no_go_risks_resolved == true AND legal.status IN ['agreement_path_approved', 'executed'] AND compliance_risk.preliminary_approval_granted == true
minimum_to_unblock_met_formula.when_DP4_is_no: security_privacy.preliminary_approval_granted == true AND security_privacy.no_go_risks_resolved == true AND legal.status IN ['agreement_path_approved', 'executed']

go_live_criteria_met_formula.when_DP4_is_yes_and_DP2_has_financial_mo: security_privacy.final_approval_granted == true AND legal.status == 'executed' AND compliance_risk.final_approval_granted == true AND commercial_finance.economics_confirmed == true
go_live_criteria_met_formula.when_DP4_is_yes_and_no_financial_motion: security_privacy.final_approval_granted == true AND legal.status == 'executed' AND compliance_risk.final_approval_granted == true
go_live_criteria_met_formula.when_DP4_is_no_and_DP2_has_financial_mot: security_privacy.final_approval_granted == true AND legal.status == 'executed' AND commercial_finance.economics_confirmed == true

integration_guide_complete.when_active: Field required — integration guide is a mandatory launch kit deliverable
integration_guide_complete.when_inactive: Field absent — no technical integration to document

certification_evidence_confirmed.when_active: Field required — certification evidence must be confirmed before Step 6 completes
certification_evidence_confirmed.when_inactive: Field absent — no integration certification required

marketplace_listing_ready.when_active: Field required — listing metadata, positioning, and compatibility notes must be complete
marketplace_listing_ready.when_inactive: Field absent — no marketplace motion selected

marketplace_listing_approved.when_active: Field required — approval from marketplace provider must be confirmed

brand_legal_compliance_approved.when_active: Field required (not optional) — public-facing joint assets require brand/legal/compliance approval
brand_legal_compliance_approved.when_inactive: Field present but optional — applies only if launch comms involve public-facing claims

rollback_plan_ready.when_active: Field required — rollback plan must be documented and ready before go-live
rollback_plan_ready.when_inactive: Field absent — no technical cutover to roll back

rollback_executed.when_active: Field exists — records whether rollback was executed

health_metrics.uptime.when_inactive: Field absent — no technical integration to monitor

health_metrics.volume.note: Always present — commercial volume tracked regardless of whether technical integration exists

payout_mechanics_ready.when_active: Field required. Hard gate: step9_readiness cannot reach 'ready' until this = true
payout_mechanics_ready.when_inactive: Field absent — no financial motion selected; RevOps gate to Step 9 does not apply

step9_readiness_formula.when_financial_motion_selected: stability_status == 'stable' AND support_burden_level == 'acceptable' AND activation_signals_met == true AND payout_mechanics_ready == true
step9_readiness_formula.when_no_financial_motion: stability_status == 'stable' AND support_burden_level == 'acceptable' AND activation_signals_met == true

co_sell.pipeline_review_cadence_required.when_active: Weekly pipeline review cadence is required (not optional)
co_sell.pipeline_review_cadence_required.when_inactive: Pipeline review cadence is recommended but not required

co_sell.close_plan_owner_value.when_direction_is_jointly_led: close_plan_owner = 'joint' — credit split rules must be defined

expansion.deeper_integration_depth.when_active: Field present — new endpoints/modules are a valid expansion play
expansion.deeper_integration_depth.when_inactive: Field absent — no technical integration to expand

progression_gate_rules._description: Explicit conditions that must evaluate to true for progression from one step to the next. These are the machine-checkable versions of the handoff criteria in the v14 document.

gate_OS_to_step1.description: Partner Operating System must be current before Step 1 can proceed

gate_step1_to_step2.description: Step 1 complete — partner record has intake decision and owner assigned
gate_step1_to_step2.condition: partner_record.intake_decision IN ['engage', 'decline', 'park'] AND partner_record.owner != null AND partner_record.lifecycle_status != 'pending_os'

gate_step2_to_step3.description: Step 2 complete — partner profile has track, tier, entitlements, gates, and engagement model
gate_step2_to_step3.condition: partner_profile.profile_status == 'current' AND partner_profile.track != null AND partner_profile.tier != null AND partner_profile.engagement_model != null
gate_step2_to_step3.when_DP1_is_no_integration: Handoff goes directly to Step 4 (Step 3 skipped)
blocked_by: unmet_access_prerequisites == true (security/compliance gate or contracting prerequisite not satisfied)

DP3 IN ['partner_cert_only', 'both'] AND certification_as_prerequisite_to_step3 == true AND certification_record.partner_cert.status != 'certified' (partner must achieve certification before proceeding, or certification may be set as concurrent — determined by program configuration)

gate_step2_to_step4_no_integration.description: When DP1 = no_integration, Step 2 handoff goes directly to Step 4
gate_step2_to_step4_no_integration.note: Integration Plan/Spec object is not created. Approval Record is created with reduced Security & Privacy track scope.

gate_step3_to_step4.description: Step 3 complete — integration plan/spec is approved and partner has confirmed intent
blocked_by: integration_plan_spec.spec_status == 'rejected' (no path forward — partner exits or parks)
DP4 == 'yes' AND compliance_risk_prohibited_use_identified == true (blocked until rescoped)

gate_step4_to_step5.description: Step 4 minimum-to-unblock criteria met — implementation may begin
gate_step4_to_step5.minimum_to_unblock_formula_when_DP4_yes: security_privacy.preliminary_approval_granted == true AND security_privacy.no_go_risks_resolved == true AND legal.status IN ['agreement_path_approved', 'executed'] AND compliance_risk.preliminary_approval_granted == true
gate_step4_to_step5.minimum_to_unblock_formula_when_DP4_no: security_privacy.preliminary_approval_granted == true AND security_privacy.no_go_risks_resolved == true AND legal.status IN ['agreement_path_approved', 'executed']
gate_step4_to_step5.when_DP1_is_no_integration: Step 5 is skipped — gate_step4_to_step6_no_implementation applies instead
blocked_by: any_track.status == 'escalated' (Step 5 work paused until escalation resolves)

gate_step4_to_step6_no_implementation.description: When DP1 = no_integration, Step 4 handoff goes directly to Step 6 (Step 5 skipped)
gate_step4_to_step6_no_implementation.note: Full go-live criteria required before Step 6 begins, since there is no implementation step to bridge between approvals and launch readiness.

gate_step5_to_step6.description: Step 5 complete — integration meets spec and launch-readiness inputs are provided
gate_step5_to_step6.condition: DP1 != 'no_integration' AND launch_readiness_package.integration_meets_spec == true AND launch_readiness_package.test_results_provided == true AND launch_readiness_package.known_limitations_documented == true AND launch_readiness_package.support_contacts_confirmed == true

gate_step6_to_step7.description: Step 6 complete — launch artifacts are complete and go-live prerequisites are satisfied
gate_step6_to_step7.go_live_criteria_formula_when_DP4_yes_an: security_privacy.final_approval_granted == true AND legal.status == 'executed' AND compliance_risk.final_approval_granted == true AND commercial_finance.economics_confirmed == true
gate_step6_to_step7.go_live_criteria_formula_when_DP4_yes_no: security_privacy.final_approval_granted == true AND legal.status == 'executed' AND compliance_risk.final_approval_granted == true
gate_step6_to_step7.go_live_criteria_formula_when_DP4_no_and: security_privacy.final_approval_granted == true AND legal.status == 'executed' AND commercial_finance.economics_confirmed == true

gate_step7_to_step8.description: Step 7 complete — hypercare exit criteria met and formal handoff confirmed
gate_step7_to_step8.prerequisites: operational_handoff_package.hypercare_exit_criteria_met == true AND operational_handoff_package.runbooks_confirmed == true AND operational_handoff_package.contacts_confirmed == true AND no_unresolved_critical_issues == true
blocked_by: operational_handoff_package.handoff_status == 'rolled_back' (must return to Step 5 and Step 6 first)

gate_step8_to_step9.description: Step 8 activation signals met and Step 9 readiness confirmed
gate_step8_to_step9.step9_readiness_formula_with_financial_m: operations_record.stability_status == 'stable' AND operations_record.support_burden_level == 'acceptable' AND operations_record.activation_signals_met == true AND operations_record.payout_mechanics_ready == true
gate_step8_to_step9.step9_readiness_formula_no_financial_mot: operations_record.stability_status == 'stable' AND operations_record.support_burden_level == 'acceptable' AND operations_record.activation_signals_met == true

gate_step9_to_step10.description: Step 9 complete — plays executed, outcomes recorded, post-play review done

gate_step10_completion.description: Step 10 review complete — decision recorded with rationale and owners
gate_step10_completion.condition: lifecycle_decision_record.review_status == 'complete' AND lifecycle_decision_record.decision != null AND lifecycle_decision_record.rationale != null

state_transition_rules._description: Explicit rules governing state transitions for each object. Each rule specifies the trigger condition and the resulting state.

partner_record.lifecycle_status.trigger: DP1 != 'no_integration' AND approval_record.minimum_to_unblock_met == true AND step4_to_step5_handoff_accepted == true
DP1 == 'no_integration' AND approval_record.go_live_criteria_met == true AND step4_to_step6_handoff_accepted == true
launch_readiness_package.package_status == 'complete' AND approval_record.go_live_criteria_met == true AND step6_to_step7_handoff_accepted == true

partner_record.lifecycle_status.note: 'renewed' is a transient state confirming the renew decision before returning to active operations'}
Placeholder: transitions back to whatever lifecycle_status was active before the block was triggered. Not a literal state value — the system must track the pre-block state to restore it.
Can occur from Steps 3, 4, 5 (unresolved blocks) or Step 10 (lifecycle decision)

integration_plan_spec.spec_status.trigger: material_requirement_changes == true OR any_step4_track_imposes_scope_changing_conditions == true OR step5_uncovers_scope_gaps == true

approval_record.security_privacy.status.note: Track is present but scope is minimal — limited to data handling obligations in the legal agreement only. No data-flow review, auth/encryption controls, or security testing.

approval_record.legal.status.trigger: legal_approves_agreement_path == true AND template_selected == true AND redline_boundaries_agreed == true

operational_handoff_package.handoff_stat.trigger: formal_handoff_confirmation_from_build_team == true AND support_operations_accepts_handoff == true AND runbooks_confirmed == true AND contacts_confirmed == true AND no_unresolved_critical_issues == true

operations_record.step9_readiness.trigger_no_financial_motion: stability_status == 'stable' AND support_burden_level == 'acceptable' AND activation_signals_met == true
operations_record.step9_readiness.trigger_with_financial_motion: stability_status == 'stable' AND support_burden_level == 'acceptable' AND activation_signals_met == true AND payout_mechanics_ready == true

lifecycle_decision_record.review_status.trigger: decision_recorded == true AND rationale_documented == true AND owners_assigned == true

growth_plan.play_status.note: Play does not exist in this configuration

growth_plan.play_status.trigger: DP2_motion_for_this_play_selected == true AND growth_plan.plan_status == 'active' AND play_not_yet_initiated == true

lifecycle_decision_record.decision.trigger: review_outcome IN ['invest', 'maintain'] AND renewal_terms_agreed == true AND lifecycle_decision_record.review_status == 'complete'
lifecycle_decision_record.decision.what_happens_next: Renewal paperwork executed. partner_record.lifecycle_status returns to operations.

lifecycle_decision_record.decision.trigger: re_tiering_warranted == true AND new_tier_criteria_met == true AND lifecycle_decision_record.review_status == 'complete'
lifecycle_decision_record.decision.what_happens_next: partner_profile.profile_status transitions to under_revision. New entitlements agreed.
Remediation plan issued with milestones and measurable criteria. Re-evaluation date set. May trigger return to Step 3 if integration changes required.
Exit plan executed. partner_record.lifecycle_status transitions to exited.

workflow_modification_rules._description: Rules governing how step content and ownership are modified based on decision point answers. These are not object/field rules — they describe how the human-readable workflow instructions change.

step3_scoping_ownership.when_DP1_is_entity_to_partner: Scoping centers on partner APIs and endpoints. Entity engineering team owns build commitment.
step3_scoping_ownership.when_DP1_is_partner_to_entity: Scoping centers on entity APIs and endpoints. Partner engineering team owns build. Entity Solutions Engineering scopes and commits only.
step3_scoping_ownership.when_DP1_is_bidirectional: Scoping covers both directions simultaneously. Both build streams must be defined.

step5_implementation_ownership.when_DP1_is_entity_to_partner: Entity leads implementation. Partner provides access credentials, sandbox, and API documentation.
step5_implementation_ownership.when_DP1_is_partner_to_entity: Partner leads implementation. Entity provides access credentials, sandbox, and API documentation. Evidence collection is partner's responsibility, validated by entity.
step5_implementation_ownership.when_DP1_is_bidirectional: Two parallel build streams coordinated. Both parties lead their respective implementations.

step4_legal_agreement_type.when_reseller_partner_selected: legal.agreement_type = 'reseller_agreement' — covers resale rights, territory, pricing floors
step4_legal_agreement_type.when_marketplace_third_party_selected: Additional marketplace platform agreement required (e.g. AWS Marketplace Seller Agreement, Microsoft Publisher Agreement) in addition to partner agreement

step6_launch_kit_content.when_DP1_is_no_integration: Launch kit covers commercial motion only — referral submission process, reseller ordering, marketplace listing usage. No integration guide.
step6_launch_kit_content.when_DP1_has_integration: Launch kit includes integration guide, technical documentation, and known technical limitations in addition to commercial content.
step6_launch_kit_content.when_DP3_includes_partner_cert: Launch kit includes certification program materials — training content, assessment details, certification pathway, badge information.

step7_hypercare_scope.when_DP1_is_no_integration: Hypercare monitors commercial motion activation only — first referral submitted, first listing live, first reseller order. No technical rollback plan.
step7_hypercare_scope.when_DP1_has_integration: Full technical hypercare — monitoring, rapid triage, rollback plan readiness. Engineering leads.

step8_health_metrics_scope.when_DP1_is_no_integration: Health metrics focus on commercial indicators — referral quality, pipeline accuracy, listing performance, support burden.
step8_health_metrics_scope.when_DP1_has_integration: Health metrics include uptime, error rates, volume, and latency in addition to commercial indicators.

step9_co_sell_account_ownership.when_direction_is_entity_led: Account ownership resides with entity. Partner has no independent claim. Credit: entity-sourced, partner-influenced.
step9_co_sell_account_ownership.when_direction_is_partner_led: Account ownership resides with partner for duration of deal. Rules for post-deal account access must be defined. Credit: partner-sourced, entity-influenced. Account conflict risk: highest — explicit rules required if entity direct sales encounters same account.
step9_co_sell_account_ownership.when_direction_is_jointly_led: Account ownership is shared. Rules must address renewal, upsell, and support escalation ownership post-deal. Credit: jointly sourced — split rules defined upfront in CRM/PRM. Weekly pipeline review required.

step9_expansion_play_scope.when_DP1_is_no_integration: Expansion plays: new use case activation, new segment rollout, enablement refresh. 'Deeper integration depth (additional endpoints/modules)' is absent.
step9_expansion_play_scope.when_DP1_has_integration: All expansion plays available including deeper integration depth.

step10_finance_revops_authority.when_financial_motion_selected: Finance/RevOps has final decision authority on economics viability at renewal/re-tier/exit review.
step10_finance_revops_authority.when_no_financial_motion: Finance/RevOps authority at Step 10 is limited to general commercial constraints — no payout economics to adjudicate.

step2_entitlements_by_motion._description: Step 2 entitlement assignments vary by selected commercial motion. Each motion adds specific entitlements to the partner profile.
step2_entitlements_by_motion.when_referral_inbound_selected: Partner receives referral tracking access and lead submission mechanism. Referral entitlements recorded in partner_profile.entitlements.
step2_entitlements_by_motion.when_referral_outbound_selected: Entity referral process defined — how entity identifies and passes leads to partner. Reverse referral terms recorded in partner_profile.entitlements.
step2_entitlements_by_motion.when_reseller_partner_selected: Partner receives margin/discount structure, deal registration access, and reseller-specific SLAs. Reseller entitlements recorded in partner_profile.entitlements.
step2_entitlements_by_motion.when_reseller_entity_selected: Entity receives access to partner product for resale. Margin/commission structure from partner to entity recorded in partner_profile.entitlements.
step2_entitlements_by_motion.when_marketplace_any_selected: Listing entitlements assigned — marketplace access, listing slot, and any platform-specific prerequisites recorded in partner_profile.entitlements.
step2_entitlements_by_motion.when_co_sell_selected: Co-sell entitlements assigned — account mapping access, joint pipeline tooling access, and lead-sharing rights recorded in partner_profile.entitlements.
step2_entitlements_by_motion.when_co_marketing_selected: Co-marketing eligibility flag set (partner_profile.entitlements.co_marketing_eligible = true). Budget access and campaign participation rights recorded.

step9_referral_attribution_direction._description: When both referral_inbound and referral_outbound are selected simultaneously, attribution mechanics must distinguish between the two flows to prevent double-counting and reporting confusion.
step9_referral_attribution_direction.when_only_referral_inbound_selected: Attribution tracks partner-to-entity referrals only. Credit definitions: partner-sourced. Single attribution flow — no directional disambiguation required.
step9_referral_attribution_direction.when_only_referral_outbound_selected: Attribution tracks entity-to-partner referrals only. Credit definitions: entity-sourced. Reverse referral fee or quid pro quo tracked. Single attribution flow.
step9_referral_attribution_direction.when_both_referral_directions_selected: Attribution mechanics must explicitly tag each credit record with direction: partner_to_entity OR entity_to_partner. CRM/PRM system fields must include a direction field on every referral credit record. Reporting must aggregate by direction separately before combining totals. Payout triggers and schedules may differ by direction and must be defined independently. Dispute process must specify which direction the dispute applies to.
